Free Career Roadmaps, CV Builder and Interview Prep

Career2Day helps learners move from lost to job-ready with free career roadmaps, interview preparation, quizzes, project guidance, readiness tracking, and a role-aware CV builder.

What is Career2Day?

Career2Day is a free career readiness platform for learners who want more than a list of tutorials. It connects role-specific career roadmaps, interview practice, quizzes, project guidance, readiness tracking, and a browser-based CV builder so every step supports the same job goal.

Start by choosing a target career such as AI Engineer, Frontend Engineer, Data Analyst, or Data Scientist. Each path shows the skills to learn, tools to practise, projects to build, and interview topics to review. As you complete work, Career2Day helps turn that proof into stronger CV sections, clearer project bullets, and better answers for recruiters and hiring managers.

What Career2Day offers

  • Step-by-step career roadmaps for technology roles.
  • Interview questions and quiz practice connected to each career path.
  • A browser-based CV builder that helps turn skills and projects into application-ready content.
  • Latest career guides about learning, portfolios, ATS keywords, interviews, and first-role preparation.
  • Weekly career tips for roadmap progress, CV improvements, interview prep, and portfolio ideas.

Featured career paths

  • AI Engineer: AI Engineers design, evaluate, ship, and monitor AI-powered features. The role blends machine learning fundamentals, software engineering, data quality, model evaluation, LLM workflows, and responsible AI judgment.
  • Machine Learning Engineer: Machine Learning Engineers turn data and modeling ideas into production systems. They combine software engineering, statistics, model evaluation, data pipelines, deployment, and monitoring.
  • Generative AI Engineer: Generative AI Engineers design AI workflows around large language models, retrieval systems, agents, evaluation sets, guardrails, and product integration. The role is closer to applied software engineering than prompt tinkering.
  • AI Research Scientist: AI Research Scientists investigate new algorithms, architectures, training methods, evaluation techniques, and theoretical questions. The role requires strong math, experimentation, literature review, and communication.
  • Data Scientist: Data Scientists analyze complex datasets, design experiments, build predictive models, and communicate recommendations. The role sits between analytics, statistics, product thinking, and machine learning.
  • Frontend Engineer: Frontend Engineers own the user-facing layer of software products. They combine React, browser fundamentals, design systems, API integration, accessibility, performance, and product judgment.
  • Data Engineer: Data Engineers build batch and streaming pipelines, model data for analytics, manage orchestration, and improve data quality, reliability, and cost.
  • Analytics Engineer: Analytics Engineers combine data modeling, SQL, dbt, metric definitions, BI workflows, and stakeholder communication to make company data usable.
  • MLOps Engineer: MLOps Engineers connect ML, DevOps, data pipelines, model registries, CI/CD, infrastructure, and observability so models can run safely in production.
  • Computer Vision Engineer: Computer Vision Engineers work with image data, deep learning, annotation pipelines, model evaluation, edge constraints, and deployment workflows.
  • NLP Engineer: NLP Engineers combine text preprocessing, embeddings, transformers, evaluation, retrieval, and deployment to create language-aware applications.
  • Cloud Architect: Cloud Architects solve role-specific problems using cloud strategy, network architecture, identity and access, resilience design and tools such as AWS, Azure, GCP, Terraform.
  • Cloud Engineer: Cloud Engineers solve role-specific problems using Linux, cloud networking, IAM, compute services and tools such as AWS, Azure, Linux, Terraform.
  • DevOps Engineer: DevOps Engineers solve role-specific problems using Linux, CI/CD, containers, infrastructure as code and tools such as Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, Terraform.
  • Site Reliability Engineer: Site Reliability Engineers solve role-specific problems using service reliability, SLOs and SLIs, incident response, observability and tools such as Prometheus, Grafana, Kubernetes, Terraform.
  • Platform Engineer: Platform Engineers solve role-specific problems using developer platforms, Kubernetes, CI/CD templates, infrastructure APIs and tools such as Kubernetes, Backstage, Terraform, GitHub Actions.
  • Solutions Architect: Solutions Architects solve role-specific problems using requirements discovery, system design, cloud services, integration design and tools such as AWS, Azure, Lucidchart, Postman.
  • Enterprise Architect: Enterprise Architects solve role-specific problems using enterprise strategy, capability mapping, governance, integration architecture and tools such as ArchiMate, TOGAF, LeanIX, Lucidchart.
  • Software Architect: Software Architects solve role-specific problems using system design, domain modeling, API design, scalability and tools such as C4 Model, OpenAPI, PostgreSQL, Kubernetes.
  • Backend Engineer: Backend Engineers solve role-specific problems using API design, databases, authentication, server-side programming and tools such as Node.js, Python, PostgreSQL, Redis.
  • Full Stack Engineer: Full Stack Engineers solve role-specific problems using frontend development, backend APIs, databases, authentication and tools such as React, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Tailwind CSS.
  • Mobile App Developer: Mobile App Developers solve role-specific problems using mobile UI, navigation, local storage, API integration and tools such as React Native, Expo, Flutter, Firebase.
  • iOS Developer: iOS Developers solve role-specific problems using Swift, SwiftUI, UIKit basics, iOS navigation and tools such as Swift, SwiftUI, Xcode, Core Data.
  • Android Developer: Android Developers solve role-specific problems using Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, Android lifecycle, Room database and tools such as Kotlin, Android Studio, Jetpack Compose, Room.
  • Blockchain Engineer: Blockchain Engineers solve role-specific problems using distributed ledgers, smart contracts, cryptography basics, wallet integration and tools such as Solidity, Ethereum, Hardhat, Ethers.js.
  • Smart Contract Developer: Smart Contract Developers solve role-specific problems using Solidity, contract testing, access control, token standards and tools such as Solidity, Foundry, Hardhat, OpenZeppelin.
  • Cybersecurity Engineer: Cybersecurity Engineers solve role-specific problems using network security, endpoint security, vulnerability management, SIEM and tools such as Splunk, Wireshark, Nmap, Burp Suite.
  • Security Architect: Security Architects solve role-specific problems using security architecture, threat modeling, zero trust, IAM design and tools such as OWASP, MITRE ATT&CK, AWS IAM, Azure AD.
  • Cloud Security Engineer: Cloud Security Engineers solve role-specific problems using cloud IAM, network security, CSPM, container security and tools such as AWS IAM, Azure Security Center, Terraform, Kubernetes.
  • Penetration Tester: Penetration Testers solve role-specific problems using reconnaissance, web app testing, network testing, privilege escalation and tools such as Kali Linux, Burp Suite, Nmap, Metasploit.
  • SOC Analyst: SOC Analysts solve role-specific problems using alert triage, SIEM search, log analysis, incident escalation and tools such as Splunk, Microsoft Sentinel, Wireshark, VirusTotal.
  • Application Security Engineer: Application Security Engineers solve role-specific problems using secure code review, threat modeling, SAST/DAST, API security and tools such as Burp Suite, Semgrep, Snyk, OWASP ZAP.
  • CISO: CISOs solve role-specific problems using security strategy, risk management, governance, incident leadership and tools such as NIST CSF, ISO 27001, GRC platforms, SIEM dashboards.
  • Technical Product Manager: Technical Product Managers solve role-specific problems using product discovery, technical requirements, API understanding, roadmapping and tools such as Jira, Figma, Postman, Amplitude.
  • AI Product Manager: AI Product Managers solve role-specific problems using AI product strategy, model limitations, evaluation design, data requirements and tools such as OpenAI API, Weights & Biases, Figma, Amplitude.
  • Product Manager: Product Managers solve role-specific problems using customer discovery, prioritization, roadmapping, analytics and tools such as Jira, Figma, Amplitude, Mixpanel.
  • Engineering Manager: Engineering Managers solve role-specific problems using people management, delivery planning, technical judgment, hiring and tools such as Jira, GitHub, Linear, Confluence.
  • Software Engineering Manager: Software Engineering Managers solve role-specific problems using software delivery, technical planning, code review culture, team coaching and tools such as GitHub, Jira, Linear, Datadog.
  • Data Product Manager: Data Product Managers solve role-specific problems using data strategy, metric design, analytics products, data governance and tools such as SQL, Looker, Tableau, dbt.
  • UX/UI Designer: UX/UI Designers solve role-specific problems using visual design, wireframing, prototyping, design systems and tools such as Figma, FigJam, Maze, Miro.
  • UX Researcher: UX Researchers solve role-specific problems using research planning, interviewing, survey design, usability testing and tools such as Dovetail, UserTesting, Maze, FigJam.
  • Product Designer: Product Designers solve role-specific problems using product thinking, interaction design, prototyping, user flows and tools such as Figma, FigJam, Framer, Maze.
  • Database Administrator: Database Administrators solve role-specific problems using SQL administration, backup and restore, performance tuning, permissions and tools such as PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, pgAdmin.
  • Database Engineer: Database Engineers solve role-specific problems using database design, query optimization, schema migration, replication and tools such as PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, MongoDB.
  • Big Data Engineer: Big Data Engineers solve role-specific problems using distributed processing, Spark, data lakes, batch pipelines and tools such as Spark, Databricks, Kafka, Airflow.
  • Robotics Engineer: Robotics Engineers solve role-specific problems using robot kinematics, sensors, control systems, ROS and tools such as ROS 2, Python, C++, Gazebo.
  • Embedded Systems Engineer: Embedded Systems Engineers solve role-specific problems using C/C++, microcontrollers, RTOS basics, hardware interfaces and tools such as C, C++, STM32, Arduino.
  • AR/VR Developer: AR/VR Developers solve role-specific problems using 3D interaction, spatial UI, Unity development, performance optimization and tools such as Unity, C#, Unreal Engine, Blender.
  • Game Developer: Game Developers solve role-specific problems using game loops, physics, input systems, level design and tools such as Unity, C#, Unreal Engine, C++.
  • QA Automation Engineer: QA Automation Engineers solve role-specific problems using test planning, UI automation, API testing, CI integration and tools such as Playwright, Cypress, Selenium, Postman.

Frequently asked questions

Is Career2Day free?

Yes. Career2Day gives learners free access to career roadmaps, interview preparation, quizzes, readiness tracking, and the browser-based CV builder.

Who is Career2Day for?

Career2Day is built for students, career switchers, and early-career professionals who want a clearer route from learning to portfolio proof and job applications.

How does the CV builder connect to roadmaps?

Roadmaps help you build skills and projects, then the CV builder turns that work into role-aware summaries, skills, project bullets, and ATS-friendly wording.

What careers can I plan on Career2Day?

Career2Day includes technology career tracks such as AI Engineer, Frontend Engineer, Data Analyst, Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, and more.
